question.text: Which types of mutual fund schemes are permitted to participate in Commodity Derivative Markets?

answer.text: Hybrid Mutual Fund Schemes and Multi-Asset Mutual Fund Schemes
All categories of mutual fund schemes

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI has permitted Hybrid Mutual Fund Schemes and Multi-Asset Mutual Fund Schemes to participate in Commodity Derivative Markets. Multi-Asset funds must invest minimum 10% in at least 3 asset classes. **The Trap:** Not all mutual fund categories are allowed — only hybrid and multi-asset schemes. Pure equity or debt schemes are not permitted for commodity derivatives. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series V-A (Mutual Fund Distributors) covers mutual fund categories and their investment mandates. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Multi-asset funds can invest in commodities since 2018 — this gives retail investors indirect commodity exposure through a regulated mutual fund structure.
